textDisplay: My masters is in counseling and diagnosing mental illness. I stumbled into a case that changed my life. An older woman who said she had ties to some occult practices claimed to experience paranormal activity after they moved into a new house. Later she claimed episodes of possession; her husband also verified this. During a counseling session I began to delicately ask questions in order to debunk her problem without offending her, because she sincerely believed a, "so-called" demon was behind the haunting and episodic possession. A very long story short.......during the session......something happened to the woman. Her voice changed, she began to taunt me, external phenomenon occurred in the room she could not have done.  Never in my life have I seen something like this. I was so scared my shirt was drenched with sweat and my heart was racing like a just sprinted 200yards. I didn't sleep that night bc every time I closed my eyes I relived this horror.  I will spare you the details of all that happened. I understand skeptics and people not believing my story. However I will tell you this.....it happened just as a said....and much worse. I had a form of PTSD after this. My specialty is understanding and treating mental illness.....what I saw could not be diagnosed in the DSM5 because what I experienced was not of this world. Possession is exceedingly rare I think....and yet real. Disbelieve it if you want, it is still true...I wish it wasn't.

textDisplay: Lol looks like a fun film with but quite silly in so far as the usual “based on true events” means based loosely. Appreciate the distinctive Cardinal Sarah depiction though 😂 accuracy wise, the demons are depicted as far too powerful and unafraid of the exorcist and the exorcist as personally depicted as powerful, rather than God  being powerful through any willing exorcist, and the Church is depicted as having too aggressive of internal politics. No demon would ask for an exorcist to be brought, that’s like someone asking to be arrested and brutally tortured while their plans are foiled and their victim is presumably saved or directed towards the path of salvation. Exorcism is torturous to demons. They especially wouldn’t ask for a reputable and particularly skilled exorcist whom they’ve probably be tortured by before. It’s like suicide. Also, possession of a soul is not how it works. Demons possess bodies. One consciousness cannot possess another consciousness. Possessed people become unconscious and black out while the demons control their bodies. Again, the idea that the particular exorcist is magical is ridiculous. If an exorcist was possessed by a demon, it wouldn’t be of great utility to the demon, the exorcist just couldn’t do their job properly anymore and would be averse to all the holy objects and prayers which they deal with on a day to day basis. It’s not like stealing a superpower’s nukes, it would be like stealing a superpower’s biological weapons which cannot be modified and which have been specifically bioengineered to only work against you. It wouldn’t be a useful weapon lol.

No demon would ask for an exorcist to be brought, that’s like someone asking to be arrested and brutally tortured while their plans are foiled and their victim is presumably saved or directed towards the path of salvation. If an exorcist was possessed by a demon, it wouldn’t be of great utility to the demon, the exorcist just couldn’t do their job properly anymore and would be averse to all the holy objects and prayers which they deal with on a day to day basis. textDisplay: This really has the feel of a mystery behind the horror which I love. And Russel like plays the veteran detective. This is based on a real man where he performed about 10,000 exorcists. Russel got to know the people The exorcist worked with and he said you can tell that these people truly believe they had these experiences whether you believe in it or not. I personally have had demons whisper to me vile things in a different language but I understood what they were getting at during a sleep paralysis episode. After that there’s no doubt in my mind these entities exist and when I prayed to Jesus I haven’t had an episode since. There are countless stories like mine. Look up the asbury revival exorcism and listen to the scream that comes out of the ladies mouth. It’s authentic footage. Hundreds of people were there. Evil absolutely does exist and is not subjective.

textDisplay:
well.... about religious people and MATH - i think because it is a part of science, they dont care about numbers...

In October 2000, it was reported that Gabriele Amorth had performed over 50,000 exorcisms (which ranged from "a few minutes" to "several hours" in length). In March 2010, he said that the number had increased to 70,000. By May 2013, he said he had performed 160,000 exorcisms in the course of his ministry. 

Gabriele Amorth was a diocesan exorcist for Rome, over a span of about 25 years.

IF we, calculate 160 000 divided with those 25 years 
that brings us  6400 exorcist-rituals each year.

6400 divided with 365 - so we get how many each day
= 17,53 -> 18 each day - to make it more easy...

"A few minutes to several hours" --- lets assume 30 minutes

so, 18 rituals each day, every day, non-stop during 25 years....

those 18 rituals times 30 minutes become 9 hours each day

that is UNlikely...
textOriginal:
well.... about religious people and MATH - i think because it is a part of science, they dont care about numbers...

In October 2000, it was reported that Gabriele Amorth had performed over 50,000 exorcisms (which ranged from "a few minutes" to "several hours" in length). In March 2010, he said that the number had increased to 70,000. By May 2013, he said he had performed 160,000 exorcisms in the course of his ministry. 

Gabriele Amorth was a diocesan exorcist for Rome, over a span of about 25 years.

IF we, calculate 160 000 divided with those 25 years 
that brings us  6400 exorcist-rituals each year.

6400 divided with 365 - so we get how many each day
= 17,53 -> 18 each day - to make it more easy...

"A few minutes to several hours" --- lets assume 30 minutes

so, 18 rituals each day, every day, non-stop during 25 years....

those 18 rituals times 30 minutes become 9 hours each day

that is UNlikely...

textDisplay: There is a german movie called requem which is about demonic possesion and its the only demon movie that I love because it potrays the stuff in a real way and focus on the emotional aspect. A beautiful blond college girl who possessed while shes inlove and have a bright future. Nothing haunts me more than that. I cried after that movie, I stand up and applaused by myself. Sure its because i can emmotionaly connect to this because some personal things that now I get over. But the main demon movie stuff is just fantasy. Nobody gets sad, nobody lost the one who she/he loves because of it, nobody lost the usual world that once he/she loved, nobody begs to god for to be saved by him. As an upper comment says, there is just a demon who could do anything but just cant get up from the bed. And thats because nobody really believes in it. Nor cinema crew or audience. Nobody believes nor demons or God. They just saw as a horror fantasy so they portray it as that way. There is nothing wrong with that but as well as I understand they are potraing a real people there. But the movie is just phony demonic horror movie. (which could also be good in its genre but not with the claim of being real) I could think the other way maybe if they didnt messed up with the saint. But its clear that they are just modernist cinema dudes who dont believe in anything. Just wanna say it. But still thanks to them for reminding me of that old beautiful touching movie. Love you all.

textDisplay:
Real life exorcisms take place in a sacred space.  Full manifestations do happen, but demons gain nothing in doing so. When heads start spinning and things start flying, it's because it's game over. The demon is fully exposed and can't pretend to be a mental illness any more.
Youtube is full of interviews with actual exorcists. They give fascinating accounts, but the most impressive thing you will notice is their gentle demeanor and matter-of-fact way they talk about the subject. 
Father Vincent Lambert trained as an exorcist in Rome, and on his first week of training, a girl levitated. That wasn't what impressed him. What did was the way his mentor simply gently pushed the girl down into her chair without missing a single word of his prayers.
When he's performing an exorcism and special effects occur, he treats it like a child throwing a tantrum.

Father Vincent Lambert trained as an exorcist in Rome, and on his first week of training, a girl levitated. That wasn't what impressed him. What did was the way his mentor simply gently pushed the girl down into her chair without missing a single word of his prayers.

textDisplay: As a fan of "exorcism" type movies, which are usually quite stupid and cliché, the real stories, if there are any, are usually not as fantastical as the movies. One exception being 'The Exorcism of Emily Rose' which, historically speaking, is arguably one of the most accurate exorcism movies I've seen without all the Hollywood make-believes. This one may be more like 'The Rite', where Hollywood tore that one up too, I'm afraid. I hope they atleast do this real life priest some justice.

textDisplay:
I've read one of Father Gabriel Amorth's books. They have plenty of terrifying material to work with. Also if you have done any research into the rites of exorcism you'll find his name is everywhere. Russel Crowe is a great actor, he's had some not so great roles lately. I feel like this could and should redeem that. 

There are a lot of really good books, interviews and witnesses in addition to other media types they can use for this. I hope the writers took this job seriously, Father Amorth deserves nothing less for his legacy RIP

textDisplay:
Faith is not bs, God is not Bs, the Devil and his demons are not bs. All I ever hear is “prove it” and when I do I get the same response, that’s not proof it’s bs. When ur standin at Pearly Gates for Judgment by the All Mighty and All Knowing, how will u answer if He questions u on your wickedness, or your path of sins you have been in? For those who don’t have faith wouldn’t it be smart to live your life right just in case? If you don’t believe in Heaven and Hell, God and  Devil, then what do u you believe?This is all by chance? This life , your life, jus a snap of a finger in the life of the universe is for no reason?Cmon now, it’s hard to say this, us and this universe is by luck or chance. God gave us this world PERFECT, he gave us 1 law; dont sin and we will be graced in His love for eternity. He knows we all sin, which is why Jesus was crucified. Like I said this world was perfect until MAN corrupted it.  He gave us a guard rail(10 commandments) and we “jump over the guard rail” aka we sin, we blame Him while we fall to our demise. People ask or say “if God is Great, why is their so much evil and why did he let bad things such as the Holocaust happen? Why didn’t he save the chosen people? Why do kids get cancer? Why this or why that? Once again God gave man this world PERFECT. God gave man free will. Man corrupted this world, we suffer from our own demise, or consequences from what we as man has created through sins. once again we jumped over  “guard rail”(sinned) and it’s the results of that.
I am FAR from perfect or a good Christian. Idk when the last time it was I went to church, or sat down and read my bible like I should. I sin every single day like we all do, but I know there’s a God and Devil, Greatness and Evil, Paradise and Purgatory. I could and should be a lot better person/Christian than I am. But  I DO NOT renounce Him or his word. I’ve had my faith tested numerous times, but He has always been right beside me even when I thought I was alone. It’s only when u understand faith, and give urself over time Him will u be able to look at those times u THOUGHT u were alone, when in reality He was RIGHT NEXT TO YOU the WHOLE TIME! If you don’t see it, it’s bc u choose not too recognize it and see it for its truth. God is real people, you better get ur crap together, when ur number is called theres no mulligans on judgement. How will u fair when ur being judged?

textDisplay: Okay, first off, this look way too dramatized. Now I am going to be careful with what I say because this topic is very serious. My parents have read a book written by an exorcist and they read some of the stories to me. The devil is on a leash and cannot, I repeat CANNOT do anything unless God allows it. None of the demons are allowed to kill anyone during an exorcism or the person they are possessing. The Vatican is not evil, Hollywood needs to stop butchering the Catholic faith for entertainment. Possession is not something to say "hey this is creepy let's make a movie about this". This topic needs to be handled with care because if it isn't one risks openings themselves to actual possession. This movie is going to open up people to demons, for crying out loud Hollywood! Stop making these films unless your going to tell the true story! These actors might have been exposed to demonic influence from what this trailer looks like. Since Catholicism is the one true faith, it alone has the power to exorcise demons. Jesus left his powers to the priests, and they are the only ones who can cast out demons. Demons are incredibly afraid of Jesus and especially Mary. Read the book Diary of an Exorcists it will explain what real exorcisms look like, not Hollywood ones. I recommend do your own research carefully (very carefully) using Catholic sources.

textDisplay: The character Satan or the lightbringer, Lucifer, planet venus, son of the dawn, in greek, gets a lot of bad rep. Obviously he's composite from older mythology characters in Zoroastrian religion plagiarised and embellished. In the bible he's deliberately mistranslated in Isaiah as he was blaming Babylonian astrology mythos and the character Satan only did one bad thing under God's orders. In the New Christian Testament, he seems to be the scapegoat for literally everything, even though it was God of the old testamentthat was doing all the nasty murdering, but its Jesus who reigns in hell not the devil and it is Jesus crushing the unbelievers in the bloody wine press. Strange thing is early Christians called Jesus the Lightbringer too and one of the first Christian denominations were called the Luciferians. Maybe they're related or maybe the same guy. But today Hollywood depicts Satan as either some weird guy in a suit or a screaming child tied to a bed. I bet if you go back a few hundred years, Christians will say what the f*ck is this??? I wish they would a real honest movie about the history of Satan and the character's origins, not another of this child throwing up, possession crap, but I think if they did, they would realise the whole premise of the story of Satan is pretty dumb and man made.

textDisplay: Ugggh I was hoping they would stick to a more “realistic” approach when I heard about the movie. First of all there is no “Pope’s Exorcist” just sanctioned exorcists based out of different Diocese. Secondly Priests exert and have certain amount of defense against demons and or Satan. They can’t directly harm the priest like the trailer is showing but they can spiritually test them. Thirdly a demon will never, ever ask to be in the presence of a Priest.  The whole point of the demon possessing the victim is to keep them as spiritually distant from God as possible so inviting a holy man to come see them and help drive them completely out is the opposite of what it’s do.
textOriginal: Ugggh I was hoping they would stick to a more “realistic” approach when I heard about the movie. First of all there is no “Pope’s Exorcist” just sanctioned exorcists based out of different Diocese. Secondly Priests exert and have certain amount of defense against demons and or Satan. They can’t directly harm the priest like the trailer is showing but they can spiritually test them. Thirdly a demon will never, ever ask to be in the presence of a Priest.  The whole point of the demon possessing the victim is to keep them as spiritually distant from God as possible so inviting a holy man to come see them and help drive them completely out is the opposite of what it’s do.

textDisplay:
I read many books of Father Amorth in the original language (italian, I am italian too). In this trailer the Father that flies back just opening the door is pure fantasy. The possessed people can fly sometimes... not the priest. Also the people who are possessed have normal features (most of the time are just people who work, live a normal life but suffer in secret), they don't look like phantoms or monsters... I suggest to read all his book and then see the film... but it seems like an exageration. 
Sorry for my mistakes

textDisplay:
In a 2011 interview, Fr. Gabriele Amorth said that he met with Padre Pio around 1960 and spoke with him about the Third Secret of Fatima.  Amorth stated:

AMORTH:  “One day Padre Pio said to me very sorrowfully: ‘It is Satan who has entered the bosom of the Church [THE PHYSICAL STRUCTURES] and within a very short time will come to rule a false Church.’”

QUESTION:  ‘Did Padre Pio say anything else to you about future catastrophes: earthquakes, floods, wars, epidemics, hunger…?  Did he allude to the same plagues prophesied in the Holy Scriptures?”

AMORTH: “Nothing of the sort mattered to him, however terrifying they proved to be, except for the great apostasy within the Church.  This was the issue that really tormented him and for which he prayed and offered a great part of his suffering…”

QUESTION: “The Third Secret of Fatima?”

AMORTH: “Exactly.”

(José Zavala, El secreto mejor guardado de Fátima)

Recently, Shia LaBeouf played Padre Pio in a movie. This time, Russell Crowe is playing Fr. Gabriele Amorth in a movie. Seems to be a connection and not coincidental that the 3rd secret of Fatima was the Vatican 2 council and its efforts to disguise itself as the Church, which went underground to the catacombs since the 1960s, but is still unblemished and undefiled by the impostors who stole the buildings, almost parallel to what Catholics since St. Athanasius's time in the 4th century experienced.

Some of the comments here are made by many unbelievers, don't read what they say, they are possessed by demons themselves.

Sadly, Amorth was NOT opposed to the heresies and most important innovations of the Vatican II Sect.  He was blind and in a diabolical fog.  In fact, he accepted the New Mass (Novus Ordo), the heretical documents of Vatican II, believed that JPII, etc. were actually canonized, and denied the dogma "Outside The Church There Is No Salvation" (Extra Ecclesiam Nulla Salus).  For example, he stated: “Muslims who follow their religion are in good faith, and the Lord is aware of it.” (Amorth, The Last Conversations, p. 73). That’s heresy.

Btw, this reply is in contradiction to the FBI's rant against the pre-V2 Traditional Catholic faith, when it shouldn't be since they are supposed to support the Constitution, otherwise they're hypocrites, liars and unpatriotic criminals.

textDisplay: This looks interesting enough, I guess, but since the 1973 progenitor, no film, including the original sequels, that contains the word "Exorcist" or any of its forms, tenses, or synonyms (conjuring, omen, possession, stigmata, etc.) having to do with the topic teased above has been able to hold a candle to the William Friedkin masterpiece... not even close.  I still can't sit completely through "The Exorcist" without getting up/averting my eyes/covering my ears, et. al.  That film set the bar out of reach for any others to follow on the same subject to date, at least as far as I'm concerned, and it's fifty years old in 2023.
textOriginal: This looks interesting enough, I guess, but since the 1973 progenitor, no film, including the original sequels, that contains the word "Exorcist" or any of its forms, tenses, or synonyms (conjuring, omen, possession, stigmata, etc.) having to do with the topic teased above has been able to hold a candle to the William Friedkin masterpiece... not even close.  I still can't sit completely through "The Exorcist" without getting up/averting my eyes/covering my ears, et. al.  That film set the bar out of reach for any others to follow on the same subject to date, at least as far as I'm concerned, and it's fifty years old in 2023.

textDisplay:
Ok, looking forward to seeing this movie, but I got to ask:

If a demon can possess a random person, be this powerful, and basically have a sit-down with Vatican's top priest, then why not just infiltrate every government ruling party and give them the proverbial "stop nuking yourself" treatment? I mean, with such lack of faith in that the books on exorcism have been taken out of public circulation, there surely aren't enough truly capable individuals of both citizens and certified cloth to prevent them, so the advantage is theirs under such legally fearing leadership before God!

textDisplay:
I think it's very important to know the real story of Padre Gabriele Amorth before going to see this movie. As I've seen, the movie is inspired by his real life, and there are many interviews with Don Gabriele Amorth, as well as several books he wrote years ago about his ministry.

As a Catholic Christian, I can say that I find Monsignor Father Amorth's direct testimony more important. For those who knew him, he was described as a cheerful and witty person, always ready to help others and with great humanity. The film certainly has other objectives, such as being visually striking and intriguing to attract a large number of people to the cinema and earn a lot of money. I sincerely hope that the figure of Father Amorth is faithfully portrayed, but it makes me reflect on the fact that even in the choice of the actor, someone who does not resemble him at all was selected (not to take anything away from Russell Crowe, whom I respect as an actor).
textOriginal:
I think it's very important to know the real story of Padre Gabriele Amorth before going to see this movie. As I've seen, the movie is inspired by his real life, and there are many interviews with Don Gabriele Amorth, as well as several books he wrote years ago about his ministry.

As a Catholic Christian, I can say that I find Monsignor Father Amorth's direct testimony more important. For those who knew him, he was described as a cheerful and witty person, always ready to help others and with great humanity. The film certainly has other objectives, such as being visually striking and intriguing to attract a large number of people to the cinema and earn a lot of money. I sincerely hope that the figure of Father Amorth is faithfully portrayed, but it makes me reflect on the fact that even in the choice of the actor, someone who does not resemble him at all was selected (not to take anything away from Russell Crowe, whom I respect as an actor).

textDisplay: This looks amazing.  Possession is real people. I have seen someone shapeshifted in front of me when I was in a college classroom.  There was a Goth girl that use to sit next to me in class all the time.  One day I said something to the professor and she started laughing and that is when her voice and face changed.  No one else seemed to notice it.  I was generally freaked out and froze in fear.  When she saw that I saw what inhabited her body, She then got up and left the classroom.  After that she never sat next to me again and started avoiding me.  There were times she missed class for days.  After that I remembered how odd she use to smell.  It wasn't a body odor and she always looked clean and dressed nice in her Goth fashion, but she had an odd odor about her and sometimes it seems as if she had a shadow or an out of focus mist covering her body.  Like she was out of focus.  Every time she sat next to me I always had to grab my scarf or sweater because I started freezing for some reason.  The day that thing popped out of her it all started to make sense to me.  Weird stuff started happening in my home for weeks.  I was generally freaked the hell out.  Its almost like it was mad that it exposed itself to me.  I had to blessed my home everyday until most of the weirdness stopped.  It never completely went away until we sold that condo.  There are unexplained and explained things out here people.  The Church knows it and they have the tools to expel most demons and possessed people.

textDisplay: 📌The original Exorcist can never be redone. Different writers, producers, class of actors and actresses; an entire different era. The closest sequel that held it's weight to the original was The Exorcist 3, starring George C. Scott; and I'd also add another within that list, and that would be The Exorcism Of Emily Rose. Everything  before and after that is garbage computer generated crap with bad actors. Stop with the comedy. They'll never reach the heights of The Exorcist, and it's impact on the audience.
textOriginal: 📌The original Exorcist can never be redone. Different writers, producers, class of actors and actresses; an entire different era. textDisplay: The demons won't act because they can do what they want. The demons are free to seed doubt and rebellion in men, already broken due to the original sin, and they are obsessed with God, so every action they do is a mockery of His creation. Example: levitate: I defy the feet that step on me; vomit: I throw out the food that nourishes me; walking backward and turn around heads and limbs: I play with your most beloved creation: your precious men. If not, the possessed will know and will tell the priest: You have no authority over me; that is also why when Protestants have a possession case, which is rare, they call a Catholic priest who has to request expressed permission to perform the rite. One more thing, they deeply and incessantly hate Our Lady so much they don't even want to pronounce her name.

textDisplay:
The conflict between Ukraine and Russia is primarily a political and territorial dispute, and while religion has played a role in the conflict, it is not the primary driver of the conflict.

One aspect of the conflict that has a religious dimension is the split between the Ukrainian Orthodox Church and the Russian Orthodox Church. In 2018, the Ukrainian Orthodox Church was granted independence from the Russian Orthodox Church, which had previously held authority over the Ukrainian church. This move was seen by many as a way for Ukraine to assert its independence from Russia.

The split has also led to tensions between the two churches and their respective governments. The Ukrainian government has been accused of using the split to promote Ukrainian nationalism and to distance itself from Russia. Meanwhile, the Russian government has been accused of using the Russian Orthodox Church as a tool for advancing its political interests in Ukraine.

In terms of the impact on the world, the conflict has led to increased tensions between Russia and Western countries, particularly the United States and its allies. The annexation of Crimea by Russia in 2014 was met with international condemnation and sanctions, and the conflict in eastern Ukraine has continued to simmer, with occasional flare-ups of violence.

The conflict has also had a humanitarian impact, with thousands of people killed and many more displaced from their homes. The situation in eastern Ukraine remains tense, and a resolution to the conflict seems elusive. The involvement of religion in the conflict has added a complex dimension to an already complicated situation.

There have been many wars throughout history involving countries with Christian-majority populations, some of which have been fought between Christian countries. Here are some notable examples:

Hundred Years' War - a series of conflicts between England and France that lasted from 1337 to 1453 and involved many battles, including the Battle of Agincourt in 1415.
Thirty Years' War - a conflict in Central Europe that began in 1618 and involved many European powers, including several predominantly Protestant and predominantly Catholic countries.
War of the Spanish Succession - a conflict between 1701 and 1714 fought between several European powers, including France and Spain, which were both Catholic countries.
World War I - a global conflict that involved many European countries, including several with predominantly Christian populations, such as Germany, France, and Britain.
World War II - a global conflict that involved many countries with predominantly Christian populations, including Germany, Italy, Japan, and the United States, among others.

textDisplay: He’s not all the way back, but this is def a step in the right direction for RC. Give the “Marvel” generation of audiences a spectacle without wearing tights (at least NOT in this part) all while tapping into that rich tradition of Horror. Well done to RC for taking the risk and his reps for supporting the move or at-least not getting in the way. I’ll actually go see this in the movie theatre and sit next to real people in the dark. Now, his next move has got to be the right move, and he’ll be all the way back. We’ll just have to wait and see. Also, I feel like his work is more free, more fulfilling as he journeys on in life. Nobody paid me to say this, I’m just a cinema fan. Remember those people?

textDisplay:
Why aren't satanists possessed?
Simple: because God only allows possession for the salvation of more souls.
The demon collects repeated and poor quality souls from his satanist followers, he already has them insured. He wants bright, innocent, pure souls with potential and even if they have committed sins, all souls are valid for GOD. Evil only longs for those souls, he desires them in vain. Evil can perhaps touch the physical body of the possessed, hurt him or even kill him, because he wants that soul. He will never get the souls if God intervenes.


"The devil exists, he is not a myth" Pope Francis

Gabrielle Amorth in real life was a narcissist. You should have seen how he hammed it you for the cameras. Doing posses for with crucifix. I remember one time I found a book about him. And it had a quote from him,

"I have nothing to fear from the Devil, it's the Devil who should fear me,"

I was like wow, this douchebag really thinks he's Dean Winchester 🤮 

He's dead now, but if he weren't he'd be tickled to death to know such a ridiculous movie was made glorifying him. Or that Russel Crowe of all people was playing him. In real life he was clean shaven and the total opposite of Russel Crowe. Especially physically.

textDisplay:
Despite the usual modern film tropes (lots of loud, thudding drums), cgi etc, nothing for me will match the feeling of sheer terror I felt when I watched The Exorcist when I was 17. I've never had a film that genuinely haunted me for days and weeks afterwards. And I'm not in the least bit religious - we didn't exist on this earth for 30 billion years, we're here for 70 or 80 if we're lucky and then we cease to exist. But that film (that will be 60 years old in a couple of years), together with the Tubular Bells soundtrack, is the most disturbing film I've ever seen. This here looks interesting, in the same way that The Rite did, but that's about it.
The only other film that came close for me in affecting my psyche was the first Paranormal Activity film. Often the maxim "less is more" applies - certainly to cinema.
textOriginal:
Despite the usual modern film tropes (lots of loud, thudding drums), cgi etc, nothing for me will match the feeling of sheer terror I felt when I watched The Exorcist when I was 17. I've never had a film that genuinely haunted me for days and weeks afterwards. And I'm not in the least bit religious - we didn't exist on this earth for 30 billion years, we're here for 70 or 80 if we're lucky and then we cease to exist. But that film (that will be 60 years old in a couple of years), together with the Tubular Bells soundtrack, is the most disturbing film I've ever seen. This here looks interesting, in the same way that The Rite did, but that's about it.
The only other film that came close for me in affecting my psyche was the first Paranormal Activity film. Often the maxim "less is more" applies - certainly to cinema.

textDisplay: @carzeyjaney All films about possession will undoubtedly be OTT that's the draw but I have seen the documentary where Willian Friedkin spent time with Father Amorth, most of the time the person is struggling with mental health/ inner conflicts that take on a semblance of a possessed person, but on rare occasions, real cases have presented themselves and why there was a call for such priests to exorcise. I believe there is both good and evil in this world and mostly we invoke evil ourselves as a collective, sometimes being stupid with ouija boards etc or dabbling with the black arts and with no experience. The film does remind me of the exorcist but a film that Russ is involved in I always watch. I did not like his last film - unhinged, it started good then got silly, bad lines and lost the plot. As an actor/ director / producer want to see more.
textOriginal: @carzeyjaney All films about possession will undoubtedly be OTT that's the draw but I have seen the documentary where Willian Friedkin spent time with Father Amorth, most of the time the person is struggling with mental health/ inner conflicts that take on a semblance of a possessed person, but on rare occasions, real cases have presented themselves and why there was a call for such priests to exorcise. I believe there is both good and evil in this world and mostly we invoke evil ourselves as a collective, sometimes being stupid with ouija boards etc or dabbling with the black arts and with no experience. The film does remind me of the exorcist but a film that Russ is involved in I always watch. I did not like his last film - unhinged, it started good then got silly, bad lines and lost the plot. As an actor/ director / producer want to see more.

Again, like my last comment argues, these films have become generic.

He was a decent guy.  An exorcist in the family.   Wild!

textDisplay:
Wow, this looks like one of those sub 30% movies on Rotten Tomatoes.

Showing way too much; and not leaving enough mystery for the 'demon' character. Awful Italian accent, when there are a ton of talented actors that are actually Italian. Feels way too cinematic and blockbuster-y. Not believable or realistic, despite the "Inspired by The Actual Files" title card.

Nothing will compare to the original Exorcist. Sony Pictures is probably just hoping to break even if enough people think it's half as good.
textOriginal:
Wow, this looks like one of those sub 30% movies on Rotten Tomatoes.

Showing way too much; and not leaving enough mystery for the 'demon' character. Awful Italian accent, when there are a ton of talented actors that are actually Italian. Feels way too cinematic and blockbuster-y. Not believable or realistic, despite the "Inspired by The Actual Files" title card.

Nothing will compare to the original Exorcist. Sony Pictures is probably just hoping to break even if enough people think it's half as good.

textDisplay:
Gabriele Pietro Amorth (Modena, 1 May 1925 - Rome, 16 September 2016) was an Italian priest and writer, exorcist of the diocese of Rome.
In 2017 is released a documentary directed by the well-known director William Friedkin of the film The Exorcist, which narrates the ninth exorcism of an Italian woman in the city of Venafro called "Cristina". It was one of the most terrible exorcisms ever done.
textOriginal:
Gabriele Pietro Amorth (Modena, 1 May 1925 - Rome, 16 September 2016) was an Italian priest and writer, exorcist of the diocese of Rome.
In 2017 is released a documentary directed by the well-known director William Friedkin of the film The Exorcist, which narrates the ninth exorcism of an Italian woman in the city of Venafro called "Cristina". It was one of the most terrible exorcisms ever done.

In Italy we know Father Amorth for several very questionable positions, and for how he spoke of the devil, a guest in many broadcasts, he wrote many works, one of the best:

Have a nice day ;)

textDisplay: 🤣🤣🤣🤣 and the award for worst accent goes to Russell Crowe. This looks like a straight up spoof of the Exorcist and other movies dealing with demonic possession i was expecting Charlie Sheen to show up at some point during the trailer. Other films have done it a lot better than what was showcased in this trailer the scenes with the kid who is possessed look very comedic i couldn't take them seriously at all . To tackle a film dealing with this subject matter i think you really have to expand beyond the boundaries and really deliver something truly intense and disturbing in equal measure especially after The Exorcist set the bar so high.

textDisplay: First off, Russell is stuck in his Thor Love and Thunder voice and secondly, why are these movies always exactly the same and why hasn’t the exorcism sub-genre evolved in any way since Friedkin’s Exorcist??? This movie looks so riddled with tropes that I had a hard time even making it thru the trailer and that’s too bad because, at one time, Russell Crowe was one of the most fascinating actors on the planet and now he is reduced to hamming it up in what looks like a half baked, run of the mill waste of time like this. This trailer infuriates me. Someone needs to find a way to elevate the possession film in a way that feels fresh and genuinely scary again! Every frame of this trailer feels copped from somewhere else..Am I wrong??
textOriginal: First off, Russell is stuck in his Thor Love and Thunder voice and secondly, why are these movies always exactly the same and why hasn’t the exorcism sub-genre evolved in any way since Friedkin’s Exorcist??? Every frame of this trailer feels copped from somewhere else..Am I wrong??
